notes_program_scope: This program guideline also covers Encompass and National General claims routed through the same GHRN/Allstate direct program - all handled under the 'Allstate' carrier selection in this tool.

disallowed_items.description: Axial 1HP air movers not permitted on typical water mitigation
disallowed_items.reason: Axial HP blowers should not be utilized on typical water mitigation projects. If appropriately utilized, it must be billed as a standard ¼ HP axial fan

disallowed_items.description: General vacuuming not permitted on water mitigation estimates
disallowed_items.reason: Clean-up fees are included in the labor component of tear out line items. Additional hourly clean-up and cleaning line items are, in most cases, unwarranted. General vacuuming is not permitted

disallowed_items.description: General fogging/deodorizing not allowed on water mitigation
disallowed_items.reason: General fogging and/or deodorizing is not allowed on any water mitigation estimate

disallowed_items.description: Generalized pressure washing/steam cleaning/soda blasting not allowed on water mitigation
disallowed_items.reason: Generalized pressure washing, steam cleaning of building materials, and soda blasting are not allowed

disallowed_items.description: Tools of the trade may not be billed
disallowed_items.reason: Tools of the trade include such things as thermal imaging devices, moisture meters, remote monitoring systems, work gloves, hammers, dust masks, extension cords, Tyvek suits on Cat 1 and Cat 2 losses, etc. and may not be charged

disallowed_items.description: PPE gloves/respirators are tools of the trade, may not be billed
disallowed_items.reason: PPE, such as gloves (disposable and heavy duty), full face respirators are considered tools of the trade and may not be billed. Replacement cartridges (PAPR) and Tyvek suits may be billed if utilized on Category 3 losses

disallowed_items.description: Equipment decontamination not warranted
disallowed_items.reason: CAT 3 work areas should be cleaned and disinfected prior to placing drying equipment. Therefore, the need for decontamination of equipment is not warranted

disallowed_items.description: Labor minimums not to be included on mitigation estimates
disallowed_items.reason: Do not include labor minimums on mitigation estimates

disallowed_items.description: Content manipulation hourly charges are not acceptable
disallowed_items.reason: Content Manipulation should be billed by the room size (CON ROOM / WTR BLK). Hourly charges are not acceptable

disallowed_items.description: Labor hours for travel time not permitted
disallowed_items.reason: Labor hours for travel time are not permitted

disallowed_items.description: Labor to shift items within the warehouse during storage not permitted
disallowed_items.reason: Labor hours to shift items throughout the warehouse during storage are not permitted

disallowed_items.description: Electronics/textile cleaning must be referred to an approved vendor, not billed directly
disallowed_items.reason: All electronics and textile handling is to be referred to an Allstate approved vendor - PDR is not on the vendor program for these services. The mitigation/contents vendor should not contact these providers directly with an assignment

disallowed_items.description: Mold remediation costs not to be included in the mitigation estimate
disallowed_items.reason: Remediation costs are not to be included in the mitigation estimate - consult with the Inside Technician/Adjuster and customer on remediation cost prior to the start of work

disallowed_items.description: No bid items, lump sums, or miscellaneous items on asbestos testing/abatement
disallowed_items.reason: No bid items, lump sums or miscellaneous items should be submitted on asbestos testing or abatement assignments

disallowed_items.description: Flooring repair/replacement must be referred to CCA Global, not billed by Paul Davis
disallowed_items.reason: On any Allstate repair assignment, Paul Davis documents, estimates, and addresses all damages except flooring. Flooring repair/replacement must be referred back to the claim owner/adjuster to involve CCA Global, who handles all flooring repair/replacement services. (ITEL is still required on flooring of 144 sq ft or more.)

disallowed_items.description: Subcontractor use is prohibited - all losses must be handled by Paul Davis directly
disallowed_items.reason: All losses will be handled by Paul Davis directly - the use of a subcontractor is prohibited

disallowed_items.description: Snow/ice removal from a roof must never be billed or performed
disallowed_items.reason: Do not remove, or recommend to the insured that they remove, snow/ice from the roof - this activity creates a potentially dangerous situation. A Network Contractor should not be assigned to remove snow/ice from a roof

disallowed_items.description: Individual after-hours line items are not permitted outside the single Emergency Service Call charge
disallowed_items.reason: Individual after-hours line items should not be used (i.e., tear out wet non-salvage carpet, cut/bag - after business hours). Use only standard during-business-hours line items throughout the body of the estimate - the only legitimate after-hours charge is the single Emergency Service Call

disallowed_items.description: Storage may not be billed per vault
disallowed_items.reason: Storage charges of personal property are charged by square foot per month based on volume, using climate-controlled storage pricing. Per-vault charges are not to be used

disallowed_items.description: Charges for moving employees or equipment to/from the job site are not permitted
disallowed_items.reason: Loading/unloading and truck charges are appropriate only for moving personal property. Charges for moving employees or equipment to and from the job site are not permitted

overhead_profit_restrictions.description: O&P does not apply to mitigation estimates
overhead_profit_restrictions.reason: Overhead and Profit does not apply to mitigation estimates

overhead_profit_restrictions.description: O&P does not apply to tarping/board-up estimates
overhead_profit_restrictions.reason: Overhead and Profit does not apply to tarping/board-up estimates

overhead_profit_restrictions.description: O&P does not apply to Contents estimates
overhead_profit_restrictions.reason: Overhead and Profit does not apply to Contents estimates

overhead_profit_restrictions.description: O&P on Reconstruction is not guaranteed - requires documented complexity/coordination support
overhead_profit_restrictions.reason: Overhead and Profit is not guaranteed on a reconstruction assignment. If you believe O&P is warranted, you must explain the complexity and coordination in a note in XactAnalysis, with supporting documents (schedules, timesheets, trade coordination details) uploaded. If an Adjuster wrote the estimate, do not add O&P unless you have prior approval and state who granted it

overhead_profit_restrictions.description: No O&P allowed on asbestos or lead testing
overhead_profit_restrictions.reason: No O&P allowed on any asbestos or lead testing

f9_note_requirements.description: After-hours emergency service call must be documented
f9_note_requirements.reason: Documentation of the reasons for an After-Hours call should be noted in XactAnalysis and identified again using an F9 note within the estimate
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain why the call qualifies as after-hours and the reason for the emergency response
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: After-hours emergency service call - onsite arrival at [time] on [date], outside standard business hours. See XactAnalysis notes for approval/context.

f9_note_requirements.description: Equipment Set Up/Take Down/Monitoring should be billed as one total line item with documentation
f9_note_requirements.reason: Equipment Set Up, Take Down and Monitoring charges should be billed as a total, in one line item on the estimate, based on the amount and type of equipment utilized
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Breakdown of equipment type and count used to calculate the set up/take down/monitoring total
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Equipment used: [count] dehumidifiers, [count] air movers, [other equipment]. Set up/take down/monitoring calculated per Allstate guideline minutes-per-unit table.

f9_note_requirements.description: HEPA Vac fees require documentation of when and why they were needed
f9_note_requirements.reason: Light HEPA Vac is allowed on a 'per sf' basis for Category 3 losses when the need is appropriately documented. XactAnalysis notes should explain when and why HEPA Vac fees are being included
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Loss category and reason HEPA vacuuming was necessary
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: HEPA Vac used on [square footage] SF due to Category 3 loss. See XactAnalysis notes for documentation.

f9_note_requirements.description: Significant tear out (cabinets, casings, countertops) requires prior IT approval
f9_note_requirements.reason: To the extent cabinets, door casings, countertops, etc. may need to be removed during mitigation, consider less invasive alternatives first. Prior Inside Technician approval is needed before significant tear out is conducted
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm prior IT approval was obtained and why less invasive drying methods weren't sufficient
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Significant tear out approved by IT [name] on [date]. Less invasive drying methods were not sufficient because [reason].

f9_note_requirements.description: DRYMASK items require F9 note support
f9_note_requirements.reason: Appropriate drywall masking/protection line items may be added with F9 notes to support - the masking items found in the drywall categories should be the first consideration when masking to address drywall and texture repairs
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain the extent of masking/protection needed for the drywall or texture work
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Masking required for [area/scope] to protect from drywall/texture work. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: Coats beyond the standard sealer + 2 coats must be documented
f9_note_requirements.reason: The need for any variation from the standard sealer coat plus two coats of paint (three total coats), including extra coats, should be documented in F9 notes and supported through detailed and clearly-labeled photos
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain why additional coats beyond the standard were necessary
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Additional coat(s) needed due to [reason - e.g., color change, substrate condition]. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: Dustless sanding with cabinet masking requires F9 note
f9_note_requirements.reason: Cabinet masking may be needed when sanding a kitchen to reduce micro-dust inside the cabinets; an F9 note is required when this masking is included
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain cabinet masking scope during dustless sanding
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Cabinet masking included during dustless sanding to reduce micro-dust intrusion into cabinet interiors.

f9_note_requirements.description: Capping a water line or adding a new angle stop requires an F9 note
f9_note_requirements.reason: In many cases the shut-off valve will not shut off, so the vendor may cap the line or add a new angle stop after removal. A (PLM CAPC+) F9 note should be added to explain capping the line or adding a new angle stop
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain why the line was capped or a new angle stop was added
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Shut-off valve did not fully shut off; line capped / new angle stop added to prevent further damage during cabinet/fixture removal. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: P-trap removal/reset for vanity/cabinet removal requires an F9 note
f9_note_requirements.reason: If the vanity did not have an open back, it is necessary to disassemble the p-trap to facilitate removal of the cabinet/vanity. A (PLM SNKRS+) F9 note should be added to explain
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain why the p-trap needed to be removed and reset
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: P-trap removed/reset to facilitate vanity/cabinet removal - vanity back was not open for direct access. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: Building code items need documentation of local code applicability
f9_note_requirements.reason: It is the estimator's responsibility to verify local code requirements will be enforced during repair. If unsure, document that building code costs will be included once determined, coded to Building Code coverage
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm the local code requirement and whether it will be enforced, or note that determination is pending
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Building code item included per [code/ordinance]. Coded to Building Code coverage. Confirmation of enforcement pending with local jurisdiction.

f9_note_requirements.description: Storage billing must be an open line item with F9 note and clear date range
f9_note_requirements.reason: Storage charges may be supplemented after the preliminary 8-day upload - make storage billing an open line item with an F9 note explanation, including a clear date range in MM/DD/YY format
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Date range for the storage period, in MM/DD/YY format
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Storage from [MM/DD/YY] to [MM/DD/YY]. Climate-controlled warehouse space. See photos of stored contents.

f9_note_requirements.description: CPS LAB (unboxed item tag/wrap labor) requires an F9 note explaining the time needed
f9_note_requirements.reason: Separate line items for labor to tag/wrap unboxed items (disassemble beds/furniture, apply stretch wrap, moving blankets, tagging large items) should use CPS LAB with an F9 note to explain the time needed
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain what unboxed items required tagging/wrapping and why the labor time was needed
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: CPS LAB time for tagging/wrapping unboxed items: [list items and quantities]. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: Mold inspection fees require prior approval and a 3rd party invoice
f9_note_requirements.reason: Inspection fees for mold must be approved prior to being incurred, and a copy of the 3rd party invoice should be provided and uploaded to XactAnalysis for approval
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm prior approval was obtained and 3rd party invoice was uploaded
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Mold inspection pre-approved by [name] on [date]. 3rd party invoice uploaded to XactAnalysis.

f9_note_requirements.description: Bric-a-Brac/High Density/Low Density box charges require adjuster pre-approval
f9_note_requirements.reason: Use of Bric-a-Brac, High Density or Low Density box types should not be used - any deviation requires pre-approval by the adjuster during the initial scope of work
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm adjuster pre-approval was obtained for this box type during the initial scope of work
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Bric-a-Brac/High Density/Low Density box type pre-approved by adjuster [name] on [date] during initial scope. See XactAnalysis notes.

f9_note_requirements.description: Pack-Out/Storage/POD requires documented adjuster or IT pre-approval before work begins
f9_note_requirements.reason: Adjuster approval is required prior to all Pack-Out and Storage requests, and requests to pack out must be pre-approved by the Inside Technician/Adjuster before work begins
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm adjuster/IT approval was obtained prior to beginning pack-out/storage/POD work
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Pack-out/storage/POD pre-approved by [Adjuster/IT name] on [date], prior to work beginning. See XactAnalysis notes.

f9_note_requirements.description: Supervisor/supervisory hours require pre-approval and a note explaining tasks completed
f9_note_requirements.reason: Supervisor hours apply only to complex losses requiring specialty operations or coordination of additional specialty vendors. All supervisory charges require a note explaining the supervisory tasks completed and must be pre-approved and documented by the adjuster
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Explain the supervisory tasks completed and confirm adjuster pre-approval
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Supervisor hours for [complexity/coordination reason]. Pre-approved by adjuster [name] on [date]. See XactAnalysis notes.

f9_note_requirements.description: Asbestos Full-Service Survey requires prior IT approval
f9_note_requirements.reason: Any Full-Service Surveys shall be pre-approved by an IT. Per-sample testing does not require pre-approval
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm this is a Full-Service Survey (not per-sample testing) and that IT pre-approval was obtained
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Full-Service Survey pre-approved by IT [name] on [date]. See XactAnalysis notes.

f9_note_requirements.description: Heavy clean requires advance adjuster approval and a reason note
f9_note_requirements.reason: Heavy clean must be approved in advance by the adjuster and a note provided indicating the reason it was needed
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm advance adjuster approval and explain why heavy clean (vs. standard clean) was needed
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Heavy clean approved in advance by adjuster [name] on [date]. Reason: [explanation]. See photos.

f9_note_requirements.description: Hydroxyl Generator use requires adjuster pre-approval
f9_note_requirements.reason: Hydroxyl Generator should be pre-approved by the adjuster; charges are per 24-hour period
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm adjuster pre-approval and the number of 24-hour periods billed
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Hydroxyl Generator pre-approved by adjuster [name] on [date]. Billed for [count] 24-hour period(s).

f9_note_requirements.description: Unpacking customer boxes on request requires IT pre-approval
f9_note_requirements.reason: Allstate as a standard will not pay to unpack boxes by request of the homeowner - any labor for unpacking customer items must be pre-approved by the IT and documented well
f9_note_requirements.required_info: Confirm IT pre-approval was obtained for this unpacking labor
f9_note_requirements.recommended_note: Unpacking labor pre-approved by IT [name] on [date] at homeowner's request. See XactAnalysis notes.

observations.description: General cleaning (CLN) items should not be needed on Cat 1/2 losses
observations.reason: General cleaning of unaffected fixtures, appliances, cabinetry, walls, flooring, ceilings, windows, etc. should not be required on Category 1 and 2 mitigation losses but may be necessary in a Category 3 event. Additional documentation is required if these line items are utilized
observations.recommendation: Verify this is a Category 3 loss or that additional documentation supports the need for general cleaning

observations.description: Final Clean is generally not warranted - most line items already include cleanup
observations.reason: Many Xactimate line items include time for cleanup after the operation is completed, so the final clean line item should not be required on most estimates. If included, an XactAnalysis note explaining the circumstance should be present
observations.recommendation: Verify photos and an XactAnalysis/F9 note support why final clean was needed beyond what's already included in other line items

observations.description: Hourly drywall labor generally not permitted when line items exist
observations.reason: Hourly labor is not generally permitted when line items exist in Xactimate for suitable estimating. Labor minimums will often supplement labor for smaller jobs instead
observations.recommendation: Verify no suitable Xactimate line item exists before using hourly drywall labor

observations.description: Small drywall repairs may be better handled with PNT PATCH
observations.reason: Consider using the item PNT PATCH for small holes, dents, nail pops, etc. Small drywall repairs can often be done in conjunction with painting, which may eliminate the need for a drywall labor minimum
observations.recommendation: Verify whether PNT PATCH would be more appropriate than a separate DRY PATCH line item for this repair size

observations.description: Multiple drywall patch operations in the same room may exceed labor actually needed
observations.reason: When using multiple drywall patch operations on the same estimate, verify the total drywall labor provided by individual patch operations does not exceed the amount actually necessary to complete all repairs
observations.recommendation: Verify combined labor across all drywall patch line items in this room isn't more than what's actually needed

observations.description: Baseboard and quarter round of the same color should be billed together as PNT BCS
observations.reason: When charging to paint both the baseboard and quarter round, use the PNT BCS line item. Charging to paint them separately is not allowed when they are the same color
observations.recommendation: Verify baseboard and quarter round aren't billed as two separate paint charges when they're the same color - use PNT BCS instead

observations.description: Masking/floor protection is typically already included in the paint operation's Retail Labor Rate
observations.reason: The Xactimate Retail Labor Rate for paint operations includes time for masking of trim/openings, cut-in time, and floor protection. There is typically no need to add these as separate line items in addition to the paint operation
observations.recommendation: Verify this masking/protection charge isn't already covered by the paint line item's Retail Labor Rate

observations.description: Two-color paint entry requires pre-loss photo evidence
observations.reason: Pre-loss two color selections must be photographed to show this existed prior to the loss
observations.recommendation: Verify pre-loss photos support the two-color paint scheme before including this line item

observations.description: Wood, ceramic tile, and stone flooring removal requires prior IT approval
observations.reason: Inside Technician approval is required prior to the removal of wood (solid or engineered), ceramic tile, and stone flooring to confirm necessity of removal and what should be done with the flooring sample. Carpet and laminate flooring damaged by water no longer require pre-approval
observations.recommendation: Verify prior IT approval was obtained and documented for this flooring removal

observations.description: Sanding hardwood may need dust protection or dustless sanding
observations.reason: When sanding hardwood flooring, it may be necessary to seal openings to prevent dust spread. Dustless sanding is permitted and reduces the need for additional dust control or final cleaning in most cases
observations.recommendation: Verify dust protection or dustless sanding is reflected if this sanding/refinishing scope would otherwise require a separate dust-control charge

observations.description: Debris haul quantity should match the actual volume of debris
observations.reason: If a full truck/dumpster load is not required, quantities should be adjusted accordingly (i.e., qty .25, .50, .75 of a full load) based on the volume/weight of items being disposed of
observations.recommendation: Verify the debris haul quantity reflects actual volume rather than defaulting to a full load

observations.description: HMR items should not appear on a water mitigation estimate
observations.reason: HMR line items should not be utilized on any water mitigation project - they belong on a separate Asbestos Testing/Abatement or Lead Testing assignment. This is stated directly in the Water Mitigation Requirements section, but since HMR is also legitimately used for asbestos/lead testing assignments, this flag needs manual review to confirm which type of assignment this estimate actually is - lower confidence than other rules here
observations.recommendation: Verify this estimate is an Asbestos/Lead Testing or Abatement assignment, not a Water Mitigation assignment, before allowing this HMR item

observations.description: Physically damaged kitchen cabinetry may require the itel Cabinet Repair Analysis
observations.reason: As of 3/31/25, the itel Now Mobile App Cabinet Repair Analysis should be used when kitchen cabinets only are damaged, except when there is only damage to the toe kick. If cabinets are a total loss, no app submission is needed, but IT should still be notified
observations.recommendation: Verify the itel Cabinet Repair Analysis was completed for this kitchen cabinet damage, unless it's toe-kick-only damage or a total loss

observations.description: Only one emergency service call charge is allowed per project
observations.reason: There is only one emergency service call charge per project regardless of the number of crews mobilized to the site
observations.recommendation: Verify this isn't a duplicate emergency service call charge - only one is allowed per project

observations.description: Only one content manipulation charge is allowed per room
observations.reason: Only 1 manipulation charge should be used per room, based on its size and quantity of contents
observations.recommendation: Verify this room doesn't have more than one CON ROOM/WTR BLK content manipulation charge

observations.description: Painting gutters, downspouts, vents, and drip edge is generally not warranted
observations.reason: Gutters/downspouts, vents and drip edge are available in a wide variety of colors and should only be painted if the original items were painted a unique color which is not available from the manufacturer
observations.recommendation: Verify the original color is confirmed unavailable from the manufacturer before including this paint charge

observations.description: Test cleaning requires photo documentation of the area, item, and sponge/cloth used
observations.reason: Test cleaning is required to show the extent of smoke/soot damage - photos of test cleaning must include the area of the test, the item tested, and a photo of the sponge/cloth used
observations.recommendation: Verify photos of the test area, item, and sponge/cloth are attached to support this test cleaning charge

observations.description: Antimicrobial use requires prior IT approval on Category 1 losses
observations.reason: Antimicrobial use is not permitted on Category 1 losses without prior approval by an Inside Technician, for which adequate support must be provided. This tool doesn't extract Cat 1/2/3 loss category from estimates, so this is flagged as a review reminder on any antimicrobial item rather than gated to Cat 1 only
observations.recommendation: Verify this isn't a Category 1 loss, or that prior IT approval was obtained and documented for this antimicrobial charge

notes: Blanket depreciation is not allowed (must be directed by IT/Adjuster and applied by line item) - not encoded as a rule since depreciation is an estimate-level field, not a line-item pattern this tool can check.
No opening statement should be included on the estimate, and the state-specific closing statement is required - these are estimate-header/document content, not line items, so not encoded as rules.

Contents quantities in excess of 1.0 (e.g. CON ROOM/WTR BLK billed at 1.10, 1.25, 1.50) are allowed but require photo documentation and an F9 note - not encoded as an F9 rule because the F9 check engine matches on description text only and cannot gate on the quantity value, which would cause every normal (exactly 1.0) content manipulation charge to be flagged unnecessarily.

Specialty trades (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, etc.) over $300 require IT pre-approval (Pages 8-9) - not encoded as a rule because it needs a dollar-threshold mechanism gated on the item's own total, which doesn't exist in this tool yet; encoding it as an always-fire rule would falsely flag every plumbing/electrical/HVAC line item regardless of value.

ITEL is required on carpet/vinyl/laminate/wood flooring replacements of 144 SF or more, and on anything above Standard Grade - not encoded as an observation for the same reason USAA's 250 SF ITEL threshold wasn't encoded: it would need a quantity-threshold observation mechanism (flag only when the item's own quantity exceeds a number) that doesn't exist in this tool yet.

Drying exceeding 4 days requires IT contact/documentation, and specialty equipment (AFD, etc.) has a pre-approval exemption for 1 day (Cat 1/2) or up to 2 days (Cat 3) - not encoded as hard requirements because duration/day-count isn't reliably available from a single line item's quantity field; encoding these as always-required would falsely flag equipment used well within the exemption.

Content manipulation room-size tiers (Small <100 SF, Medium 100-150 SF, Large 150-200 SF, X-Large 200+ SF) aren't verified since this tool doesn't currently extract room square footage from estimates.

Cat 1/2/3 loss category isn't a field this tool extracts from estimates, so several Cat-conditional rules (antimicrobial approval, CLN item necessity, tear-out method by category) are phrased as review reminders rather than hard flags.

Duplication of tasks between a Mitigation estimate and a Reconstruction estimate for the same job (detach/reset, removals, debris removal, sealing/cleaning) isn't checked - this tool validates one estimate at a time and has no mechanism to compare against a separate, related estimate.

Estimates should be organized Ceiling-Walls-Floor (or Floor-Walls-Ceiling) with a matching exterior order, and header info (Paul Davis logo, office name/address, franchise code) is required - these are formatting/header requirements, not line-item content.
